params.label: Yaw P-gain
params.description: Proportional gain for yaw rotation towards the target

params.label: Yaw smoothing (α)
params.description: Low-pass filter coefficient — lower = smoother

params.label: Smooth yaw
params.description: Enable exponential smoothing on yaw commands

params.label: Yaw only (no forward)
params.description: Rotate towards the target but do not move forward/backward

params.label: Max yaw rate (deg/s)
params.description: Clamp on yaw angular velocity sent to MAVSDK

params.label: Dead-zone yaw (deg)
params.description: Horizontal dead zone in degrees — target within this zone triggers no yaw

params.label: Max forward speed (m/s)
params.description: Clamp forward velocity to this limit

params.label: Max backward speed (m/s)
params.description: Clamp backward velocity to this limit

params.label: Forward smoothing (α)
params.description: Low-pass filter coefficient for forward/backward commands — lower = smoother, higher = more responsive

params.label: Smooth forward
params.description: Enable exponential smoothing on forward/backward commands

params.label: Max forward accel (m/s²)
params.description: Slew-rate cap on forward velocity — hard m/s² bound independent of max-forward and of the EMA filter. Limits PX4 pitch transients that can push the subject out of frame.

params.label: Altitude smoothing (α)
params.description: Low-pass filter coefficient for altitude hold commands — lower = smoother, higher = more responsive

params.label: Smooth altitude
params.description: Enable exponential smoothing on altitude hold commands

params.label: Distance P-gain (approach)
params.description: Approach gain on (target/bbox - 1) when factor > 0 (person too far). Lower than retreat gain by design — chasing is leisurely, retreating is safety.

params.label: Distance P-gain (retreat)
params.description: Retreat gain on (target/bbox - 1) when factor < 0 (person too close). Higher than the approach gain so retreat saturates max_backward before the bbox hits the panic safety threshold — avoids the abrupt max-retreat step.

params.label: Target bbox height (0-1)
params.description: Desired person height in frame (fraction). Drives altitude — smaller = drone flies higher, larger = flies lower. Captured from the target's current bbox at lock time so the drone holds the pilot-chosen distance; adjust here to move closer/further.

params.label: Dead-zone bbox (%)
params.description: Forward/backward dead-zone as a percentage of target bbox height. Error within this zone triggers no forward movement.

params.label: Max climb speed (m/s)
params.description: Maximum vertical speed for altitude corrections.

params.label: Min altitude (m)
params.description: Minimum allowed altitude — altitude commands are clamped above this.

params.label: Max altitude (m)
params.description: Maximum allowed altitude — altitude commands are clamped below this.

params.label: Target altitude (m)
params.description: Target altitude for flight and automated takeoff

params.label: Active tracked ID
params.description: Read-only — the person ID currently being followed (0 = none)

params.label: Follow ID
params.description: -1 = idle (hold position), 0 = auto (largest person), >0 = lock to specific ID

params.label: Auto-select on clear/loss
params.description: When on, AUTO mode re-acquires the biggest visible person whenever the target is cleared or lost. When off, the drone holds position — pilot-led workflow where the operator explicitly picks targets.

params.label: Bitrate (kbps)
params.description: x264 encoder bitrate for the drone-follow video stream. Updated by variable-bitrate automatically.

params.label: Air-side recording
params.description: Toggle air-side recording (post-overlay frames → drone_follow/recordings/*.mp4 on the drone). Available whenever drone-follow runs with --openhd-stream. Air-side recordings have fewer compression artifacts than ground-side and survive RF dropouts.

params.label: Save config (air)
params.description: Momentary trigger — set to ON to save the current controller values to df_config.json on the air unit. The toggle returns to OFF automatically once the save completes. The saved JSON is persisted on the drone's SD card and can be loaded on next boot via `drone-follow --config <path>`.

params.label: Load config (air)
params.description: Momentary trigger — set to ON to live-reload controller values from df_config.json on the air unit into the running drone-follow. All sliders refresh from the reloaded values. Toggle returns to OFF when complete. Does nothing if no saved file exists.
